Number of Arms Included.evidence: In the 'METHODS' section on page 1, it states: 'We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel ... or ADT alone.' This indicates two treatment arms.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: In the 'METHODS' section on page 1, it states: 'We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel... or ADT alone.' The first experimental arm is ADT plus docetaxel. ADT is Androgen-deprivation therapy (a type of hormonal therapy), and docetaxel is a chemotherapy agent.
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Total Participants - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'A total of 790 patients (median age, 63 years) underwent randomization.' This is further confirmed on page 4, in the 'RESULTS' section under 'PATIENTS', which states: 'From July 2006 through December 2012, a total of 790 patients were enrolled and underwent randomization.' Table 1 on page 5 also shows the breakdown as ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397) and ADT Alone (N=393), which sums to 790.

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: On page 5, Table 1, 'Baseline Characteristics of the Patients', under the column 'ADT plus Docetaxel', the total number of patients is listed as 'N=397'. This arm represents the experimental arm (ADT plus docetaxel).

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: On page 1, in the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'After a median follow-up of 28.9 months, the median overall survival was 13.6 months longer with ADT plus docetaxel (combination therapy) than with ADT alone'. On page 4, in the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'the median follow-up was 28.9 months'. Additionally, Figure 1 caption on page 6 states: 'The median duration of follow-up was 28.9 months among all patients'.

Control Arm - N.evidence: On page 5, Table 1, 'Baseline Characteristics of the Patients', under the column 'ADT Alone', the total number of participants is listed as 'N=393'.

Control Arm.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: 'We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel (...) or ADT alone.' The 'RESULTS' section on page 1 further compares 'ADT plus docetaxel (combination therapy)' with 'ADT alone'. Table 1 on page 5 also lists 'ADT Alone' as one of the study arms, indicating it is the comparison group not receiving the experimental treatment (docetaxel).

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: ADT plus docetaxel (at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area every 3 weeks for six cycles)
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: 'We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel (at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area every 3 weeks for six cycles) or ADT alone.' This describes the specific treatment protocol for Treatment Arm 1.

Treatment Arm(s).evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: 'We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel (at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area every 3 weeks for six cycles) or ADT alone.' This is further supported by the 'TREATMENT PLAN, STRATIFICATION, AND RANDOMIZATION' section on page 2 and Table 1 on page 5, which lists 'ADT plus Docetaxel' and 'ADT Alone' as the two groups.

Quality of Life Scale.evidence: The document focuses on clinical outcomes such as overall survival, time to progression, PSA levels, and adverse events. There is no mention of a 'Quality of Life Scale,' a standardized questionnaire, or any assessment of participants' overall well-being or satisfaction with their health status.

Trial.evidence: On page 2, under the 'STUDY OVERSIGHT' section, it states: 'The primary objective of the E3805 study was to determine whether docetaxel therapy at the beginning of ADT for metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer would result in longer overall survival than that with ADT alone.' This identifies 'E3805 study' as the name of the clinical study.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treat.evidence: Found in Table 1, under 'Volume of metastases - no. (%)', row 'High', and column 'ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397)'.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Contr.evidence: Found in Table 1, under 'Volume of metastases - no. (%)', row 'High', and column 'ADT Alone (N=393)'.

Volume of disease - N (%) | Low | Contro.evidence: Found in Table 1, under 'Volume of metastases - no. (%)', row 'Low', and column 'ADT Alone (N=393)'.

Quality of Life reported.evidence: The document mentions 'ECOG performance-status score' as a baseline characteristic and stratification factor (e.g., page 2, 'ECOG performance-status score of 0, 1, or 2 (on a scale from 0 to 5, with higher scores indicating greater disability)'). However, it does not report results or changes in quality of life using a dedicated quality of life assessment tool, nor does it present ECOG performance status as an outcome measure of the intervention in the 'Results' or 'Secondary End Points' sections.

Median Age (years) | Treatment.evidence: The median age for the combination group (treatment arm) is reported as 64 years in the 'RESULTS' section on page 4 and confirmed in Table 1 on page 5 under 'ADT plus Docetaxel'.

Median Age (years) | Control.evidence: The median age for the ADT-alone group (control arm) is reported as 63 years in the 'RESULTS' section on page 4 and confirmed in Table 1 on page 5 under 'ADT Alone'.

NCT.evidence: Found in the 'CONCLUSIONS' section on page 1, explicitly stated as 'Clinical Trials.gov number, NCT00309985.'

PubMed ID.evidence: The document provides a DOI (10.1056/NEJMoa1503747) and journal citation, but no explicit PubMed ID was found.

Trial Name.value: Chemohormonal Therapy in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer (E3805 study)
Trial Name.evidence: The main title of the article is 'Chemohormonal Therapy in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer' on page 1. The study is also referred to as 'the E3805 study' in the 'STUDY OVERSIGHT' section on page 2.

Author.evidence: The author list is provided on page 1, starting with Christopher J. Sweeney.

Year.evidence: The publication year is explicitly stated as '2015' in the journal citation and 'This article was published on August 5, 2015' on page 1.

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document contains extensive sections including Background, Methods, Results, Conclusions, and multiple pages of detailed information and references, indicating it is a full publication rather than just an abstract.

Phase.evidence: On page 9, in the paragraph before the references, it states that 'the definition was reproducible across two phase 3 studies conducted in a similar time period,' referring to the current study (E3805) and the S9346 trial.

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The document is titled 'ORIGINAL ARTICLE' on page 1 and presents the primary objective, methods, and results of the E3805 study, indicating it is the initial report of the trial's findings.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: The document discusses metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer and stratifies patients by volume of metastases (high vs. low), but it does not report results based on prognostic groups with synchronous metastases.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: The document discusses metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer and stratifies patients by volume of metastases (high vs. low), but it does not report results based on prognostic groups with metachronous diseases.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: Page 2, in the 'METHODS' section, states: 'stratification was performed prospectively according to high or low burden of metastatic disease.' Page 3, in 'PATIENTS' section, further clarifies: 'Patients were also stratified according to... the extent of metastases (high volume [defined as the presence of visceral metastases or ≥4 bone lesions with ≥1 beyond the vertebral bodies and pelvis] vs. low volume).' Table 1 on page 5 includes 'Volume of metastases' with 'High' as a category. Figure 1B on page 6 presents 'Patients with High-Volume Disease', and the 'SURVIVAL' section on page 6 discusses results for this subgroup: 'The benefit at the last analysis was more apparent in the subgroup with high-volume disease'. Figure 2 on page 7 also shows 'Volume of metastases' with 'High' as a subgroup for hazard ratios.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: Page 2, in the 'METHODS' section, states: 'stratification was performed prospectively according to high or low burden of metastatic disease.' Page 3, in 'PATIENTS' section, further clarifies: 'Patients were also stratified according to... the extent of metastases (high volume [defined as the presence of visceral metastases or ≥4 bone lesions with ≥1 beyond the vertebral bodies and pelvis] vs. low volume).' Table 1 on page 5 includes 'Volume of metastases' with 'Low' as a category. Figure 1C on page 6 presents 'Patients with Low-Volume Disease', and the 'SURVIVAL' section on page 6 discusses results for this subgroup: 'The median survival at the time of the analysis had not been reached in the subgroup with low-volume disease'. Figure 2 on page 7 also shows 'Volume of metastases' with 'Low' as a subgroup for hazard ratios.

PS - N (%) | 0 | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 1, 'Baseline Characteristics of the Patients', under 'ECOG performance status - no. (%)' for '0', in the 'ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397)' column.

PS - N (%) | 0 | Control.evidence: Found in Table 1, 'Baseline Characteristics of the Patients', under 'ECOG performance status - no. (%)' for '0', in the 'ADT Alone (N=393)' column.

PS - N (%) | 1-2 | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 1, 'Baseline Characteristics of the Patients', under 'ECOG performance status - no. (%)'. For the 'ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397)' column, summed values for '1' (114 (28.7%)) and '2' (6 (1.5%)) to get 120 (30.2%).

PS - N (%) | 1-2 | Control.evidence: Found in Table 1, 'Baseline Characteristics of the Patients', under 'ECOG performance status - no. (%)'. For the 'ADT Alone (N=393)' column, summed values for '1' (115 (29.3%)) and '2' (6 (1.5%)) to get 121 (30.8%).

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous.evidence: The document states that patients were enrolled with 'metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer' (page 1, Methods; page 2, Patients), implying all patients had metastases at the time of study entry. However, it does not explicitly categorize the patients into 'synchronous' or 'metachronous' groups with specific N (%) values for the treatment arm.

The document states that patients were enrolled with 'metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer' (page 1, Methods; page 2, Patients), implying all patients had metastases at the time of study entry. However, it does not explicitly categorize the patients into 'synchronous' or 'metachronous' groups with specific N (%) values for the control arm.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronou.evidence: The document does not explicitly categorize the patients into 'metachronous' metastases with specific N (%) values for the treatment arm. While it mentions 'Prior adjuvant ADT was allowed if the duration of therapy was 24 months or less and progression had occurred more than 12 months after completion of therapy' (page 2, Patients), this refers to prior treatment history, not the mode of metastasis at study entry.

The document does not explicitly categorize the patients into 'metachronous' metastases with specific N (%) values for the control arm. While it mentions 'Prior adjuvant ADT was allowed if the duration of therapy was 24 months or less and progression had occurred more than 12 months after completion of therapy' (page 2, Patients), this refers to prior treatment history, not the mode of metastasis at study entry.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states that the treatment arm received 'ADT plus docetaxel'. Table 1 on page 5 shows N=397 for the 'ADT plus Docetaxel' arm. The 'ASSIGNED THERAPY ADMINISTERED AND SUBSEQUENT THERAPY' section on page 8 specifies that '390 patients in the combination group who started the assigned therapy'. This indicates that 390 out of 397 patients in the treatment arm received docetaxel, which is (390/397)*100 = 98.2%.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states that the control arm received 'ADT alone'. Table 1 on page 5 shows N=393 for the 'ADT Alone' arm. The 'ASSIGNED THERAPY ADMINISTERED AND SUBSEQUENT THERAPY' section on page 8 reports that in the ADT alone group, '137 patients of these patients had received docetaxel for castration-resistant prostate cancer, and another 10 patients had received docetaxel before castration resistance was confirmed'. This totals 137 + 10 = 147 patients. The percentage is (147/393)*100 = 37.4%.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.evidence: The document reports median overall survival (57.6 months) and median time to progression (20.2 months) for the ADT plus docetaxel arm, and states that docetaxel was given for six cycles. However, a specific 'Median On-Treatment Duration' for the entire assigned therapy (ADT plus docetaxel) is not explicitly reported.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.evidence: The document reports median overall survival (44.0 months) and median time to progression (11.7 months) for the ADT alone arm. However, a specific 'Median On-Treatment Duration' for the ADT alone therapy is not explicitly reported.

Primary Endpoint(s).value: To determine whether docetaxel therapy at the beginning of ADT for metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer would result in longer overall survival than that with ADT alone. Specifically, to test the hypothesis that the median overall survival would be 33.3% longer among patients receiving docetaxel added to ADT early during therapy than among patients receiving ADT alone.
Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: On page 1, in the 'METHODS' section, it states: 'The primary objective was to test the hypothesis that the median overall survival would be 33.3% longer among patients receiving docetaxel added to ADT early during therapy than among patients receiving ADT alone.' On page 2, under 'METHODS' and 'STUDY OVERSIGHT', it reiterates: 'The primary objective of the E3805 study was to determine whether docetaxel therapy at the beginning of ADT for metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer would result in longer overall survival than that with ADT alone.'

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: The proportion of patients who had a decrease in the PSA level to less than 0.2 ng per milliliter at 12 months; the median time to the development of castration-resistant prostate cancer (biochemical, symptomatic, or radiographic); the median time to clinical progression.
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: On page 6, under the heading "SECONDARY END POINTS AND TOXIC EFFECTS", the text explicitly states: "The proportion of patients who had a decrease in the PSA level to less than 0.2 ng per milliliter at 12 months was 27.7% in the combination group, as compared with 16.8% in the ADT-alone group (P<0.001) (Table 2). The median time to the development of castration-resistant prostate cancer (biochemical, symptomatic, or radiographic) was 20.2 months with combination therapy, as compared with 11.7 months with ADT alone (hazard ratio in the combination group, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.51 to 0.72; P<0.001)" and "The median time to clinical progression was 33.0 months with combination therapy, as compared with 19.8 months with ADT alone (hazard ratio, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.50 to 0.75; P<0.001) (Table 2, and Fig. S2B in the Supplementary Appendix).". These are further detailed in Table 2, titled "Secondary End Points" on page 8.

No. of Deaths - N | Treatment.evidence: On page 4, under the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'the median follow-up was 28.9 months, with 136 deaths in the ADT-alone group and 101 deaths in the combination group.' The 'combination group' refers to the treatment arm (ADT plus docetaxel).

No. of Deaths - N | Control.evidence: On page 4, under the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'the median follow-up was 28.9 months, with 136 deaths in the ADT-alone group and 101 deaths in the combination group.' The 'ADT-alone group' refers to the control arm.

TTPSA (mo) | Treatment.evidence: On page 8, Table 2, under 'Secondary End Points', the 'Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer - mo*' for 'ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397)' is reported as a median of '20.2' months. The footnote indicates this includes 'serologic progression', which refers to PSA progression.

TTPSA (mo) | Control.evidence: On page 8, Table 2, under 'Secondary End Points', the 'Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer - mo*' for 'ADT Alone (N=393)' is reported as a median of '11.7' months. The footnote indicates this includes 'serologic progression', which refers to PSA progression.

Treatment Class.evidence: The title of the article is "Chemohormonal Therapy in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer", indicating the classification of the treatment. The study compares androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) alone with ADT plus docetaxel, which is a chemohormonal approach.

Type of Therapy.value: Androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) alone or in combination with ADT plus docetaxel (chemohormonal therapy).
Type of Therapy.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: 'We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel (at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area every 3 weeks for six cycles) or ADT alone.' The 'TREATMENT PLAN, STRATIFICATION, AND RANDOMIZATION' section on page 2 further elaborates: 'Patients were randomly assigned to ADT alone or to combination therapy with ADT plus docetaxel at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area given every 3 weeks for six cycles'. The title also refers to 'Chemohormonal Therapy'.

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: Found in the 'RESULTS' section on page 1 and the 'SURVIVAL' section on page 6, stating 'the median overall survival was 13.6 months longer with ADT plus docetaxel (combination therapy) than with ADT alone (57.6 months vs. 44.0 months)'. Also depicted in Figure 1A as 'median overall survival, 57.6 mo' for ADT+docetaxel.

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: Found in the 'RESULTS' section on page 1 and the 'SURVIVAL' section on page 6, stating 'the median overall survival was 13.6 months longer with ADT plus docetaxel (combination therapy) than with ADT alone (57.6 months vs. 44.0 months)'. Also depicted in Figure 1A as 'median overall survival, 44.0 mo' for ADT alone.

OS Rate (%) | Synchronous | Treatment.evidence: The document does not report OS rates specifically for synchronous metastasis in the treatment arm. It stratifies by 'high volume' and 'low volume' of metastases, and 'visceral metastases', but not by synchronous/metachronous.

OS Rate (%) | Synchronous | Control.evidence: The document does not report OS rates specifically for synchronous metastasis in the control arm. It stratifies by 'high volume' and 'low volume' of metastases, and 'visceral metastases', but not by synchronous/metachronous.

OS Rate (%) | Metachronous | Control.evidence: The document does not report OS rates specifically for metachronous metastasis in the control arm. It stratifies by 'high volume' and 'low volume' of metastases, and 'visceral metastases', but not by synchronous/metachronous.

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 2, 'Secondary End Points', under 'Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer - mo*', 'Median' row, for 'ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397)' column. The footnote for this table defines 'Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer' as the time until documented clinical or serologic progression, which aligns with progression-free survival.

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: Found in Table 2, 'Secondary End Points', under 'Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer - mo*', 'Median' row, for 'ADT Alone (N=393)' column. The footnote for this table defines 'Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer' as the time until documented clinical or serologic progression, which aligns with progression-free survival.

Median PFS (mo) | High volume | Control.evidence: The document provides median overall survival for patients with high-volume disease in Figure 1B and the 'SURVIVAL' section on page 6 (32.2 months for ADT alone), but does not report median progression-free survival specifically for this subgroup.

Median PFS (mo) | Low volume | Treatment.evidence: The document states that median overall survival was 'NR' (not reached) for patients with low-volume disease in both groups (Figure 1C and 'SURVIVAL' section on page 6), but does not report median progression-free survival specifically for this subgroup.

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.evidence: Found in Table 3, 'Adverse Events of Grade 3 or Higher among the 390 Patients Who Received the Docetaxel-Containing Regimen' on page 8. The 'Any event' row shows Grade 3 as 65 (16.7%) and Grade 4 as 49 (12.6%). Summing these gives 114 patients, which is 29.3% of the 390 patients.

On page 3, under 'MONITORING OF TOXIC EFFECTS AND EFFICACY', it states: 'Adverse events among patients assigned to ADT alone were not routinely documented, although major adverse events were to be reported.' Therefore, comprehensive all-cause Grade 3 or higher data for the control arm is not reported.

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.evidence: Found in Table 3, 'Adverse Events of Grade 3 or Higher among the 390 Patients Who Received the Docetaxel-Containing Regimen' on page 8. The 'Any event' row shows Grade 3 as 65 (16.7%) and Grade 4 as 49 (12.6%). Summing these gives 114 patients, which is 29.3% of the 390 patients. The text on page 3 states 'All grade 3 or higher toxic effects in the combination group were captured, and an attribution of relatedness to study therapy was made by the local investigators', confirming these events are considered treatment-related.

On page 3, under 'MONITORING OF TOXIC EFFECTS AND EFFICACY', it states: 'Adverse events among patients assigned to ADT alone were not routinely documented, although major adverse events were to be reported.' Therefore, comprehensive treatment-related Grade 3 or higher data for the control arm is not reported.

Found in Table 3, 'Adverse Events of Grade 3 or Higher among the 390 Patients Who Received the Docetaxel-Containing Regimen' on page 8. The 'Sudden death' row in the 'Grade 5' column shows 1 (0.3%). The text on page 7, under 'SECONDARY END POINTS AND TOXIC EFFECTS', states: 'One patient died suddenly at home of an unknown cause during the course of docetaxel therapy; the death was considered to be possibly related to docetaxel according to the Adverse Event Expedited Reporting System', confirming it as a treatment-related Grade 5 event.

On page 3, under 'MONITORING OF TOXIC EFFECTS AND EFFICACY', it states: 'Adverse events among patients assigned to ADT alone were not routinely documented, although major adverse events were to be reported.' Therefore, treatment-related Grade 5 data for the control arm is not reported.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.value: Median time to castration-resistant prostate cancer: 20.2 months (95% CI, 17.2-23.6). Median time to clinical progression: 33.0 months (95% CI, 27.3-41.2). Hazard ratio for time to castration-resistant prostate cancer (ADT plus Docetaxel vs ADT alone): 0.61 (95% CI, 0.51-0.72). Hazard ratio for time to clinical progression (ADT plus Docetaxel vs ADT alone): 0.61 (95% CI, 0.50-0.75).
PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: Found in the 'RESULTS' section on page 6 and Table 2 'Secondary End Points' on page 8. The median times for 'Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer' and 'Time to clinical progression' are reported for the ADT plus Docetaxel (Treatment) group. The hazard ratios compare the treatment group to the control group. A specific percentage PFS rate was not reported.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.value: Median time to castration-resistant prostate cancer: 11.7 months (95% CI, 10.8-14.7). Median time to clinical progression: 19.8 months (95% CI, 17.9-22.8).
PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: Found in the 'RESULTS' section on page 6 and Table 2 'Secondary End Points' on page 8. The median times for 'Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer' and 'Time to clinical progression' are reported for the ADT Alone (Control) group. A specific percentage PFS rate was not reported.
